title: On windows WSL host.docker.internal works from host to container but not from container to host
body:
### Issue Description

**inside container**
```
 wget -qO- http://host.docker.internal:11434/api/tags
wget: can't connect to remote host (10.89.1.1): Connection refused
~ $ ^C
```

**inside container using WSL IP**
```
 wget http://172.21.160.1:11434/api/tags
Connecting to 172.21.160.1:11434 (172.21.160.1:11434)
saving to 'tags'
tags                 100% |*********************************************************************************************************************************|   406  0:00:00 ETA
'tags' saved
~ $ cat tags 
{"models":[{"name":"MFDoom/deepseek-coder-v2-tool-calling:16b","model":"MFDoom/deepseek-coder-v2-tool-calling:16b","modified_at":"2026-04-09T13:48:46.9407843+01:00","size":8905126957,"digest":"ca80a6b974a447a995f6e68aa51aecb67d274adc78ef65c6b4b068c2d6bf4c94","details":{"parent_model":"","format":"gguf","family":"deepseek2","families":["deepseek2"],"parameter_size":"15.7B","quantization_level":"Q4_0"}}]}~ $ 
```

### **if you create and inspect a network EnableIPv4 is false, I think the issue is here**
I think that is the issue!
```
{
  "Name": "metalo",
  "Id": "fa58b32511195acd696223c29f11da8ca2db213c93676347747d95ed13af52a2",
  "Created": "2026-04-09T16:52:42.142965222+01:00",
  "Scope": "local",
  "Driver": "bridge",
  "EnableIPv4": false,
  "EnableIPv6": false,
  "IPAM": {
    "Driver": "default",
    "Options": {
      "driver": "host-local"
    },
    "Config": [
      {
        "Subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
        "Gateway": "10.89.0.1"
      }
    ]
  },
  "Internal": false,
  "Attachable": false,
  "Ingress": false,
  "ConfigFrom": {
    "Network": ""
  },
  "ConfigOnly": false,
  "Containers": {},
  "Options": {},
  "Labels": {}
}


```

comments.body:
Hello, can you please provide more information about your setup? It would also help to have a minimal reproducible example using something simple, like an `httpd` container.

PTAL @l0rd

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

This is something that I can reproduce too on `main` branch with the default WSL config (rootless, usermode networking disabled).

I haven't investigated the root cause or if that's expected. @Luap99 probably knows. But I have run the following commands as a workaround (I used that for the certificates import demo):

```pwsh
# get the host IP address 
$HOST_IP=$(podman machine ssh 'ip route show | grep -i default | awk ''{ print $3}''')
# add a line in /etc/hosts for `host.docker.internal`
podman machine ssh "echo $HOST_IP host.docker.internal | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts"
```

This is really a last resort workaround, as you need to run it every time the machine is restarted.

To reproduce it:
1) disable the windows firewall
1) start an http server on the host on port 8080
2) init and start a WSL machine
3) run curl or wget from a container to send a request to the server running on the host

There is no special handling for windows AFAIK, the logic is supposed to pick one host address (in this case host being the WSl VM). If WSL needs special handling to pick the right ip to reach windows host applications it must be coded into podman.

We expose `host_containers_internal_ip` containers.conf option so someone could make the machine init/start logic set that. OR otherwise add speical case directly into the resolver like we did for macos https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/638a7b8a21226864323fc1a032624d84a83e8b0c/common/libnetwork/etchosts/ip.go#L56-L60

In particular I see the following issues which I guess are related or duplicates so someone who knows WSL networking should look at this and close possible dups
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28351
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27487
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25152#issuecomment-2653572349
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24660
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21081
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
